explanation:
The requirement states that the switch to Pressure Support Ventilation (PSV) mode should occur at the end of the inspiratory phase of Pressure Controlled Ventilation (PCV), i.e., at the 'end of a PCV inspiratory time.' In the atomic propositions, 'inspiratoryPhaseEnd' indicates the end of the inspiratory phase, and 'PSVModeSelected' indicates that the PSV mode was initiated by the user (i.e., the switch to PSV). The specification should guarantee that whenever the inspiratory phase ends, the switch to PSV mode is subsequently initiated immediately after (next state). This is best expressed with template 11: G (bool_exp2 -> (X bool_exp3)), where bool_exp2 = inspiratoryPhaseEnd, and bool_exp3 = PSVModeSelected. This ensures that every time the inspiratory phase ends, the next step must be the initiation of PSV mode.
N_DURATION is not needed in this template.

The requirement states that switching to PSV (Pressure Support Ventilation) mode shall occur at the end of the inspiratory time in PCV (Pressure Controlled Ventilation). The input atomic propositions include 'PCVMode' (indicates the system is in PCV mode), 'inspiratoryPhaseEnd' (marks the end of the inspiratory phase), and 'PSVModeSelected' (indicates switch to PSV mode initiated by the user). The desired property is to ensure that switching to PSV is only possible right at the end of a PCV inspiratory period. To capture this, we want to represent: Whenever the end of the inspiratory phase in PCV mode happens, the user can (and must) select PSV mode in the next moment. This is best mapped by requiring that always, if PCVMode and inspiratoryPhaseEnd hold, PSVModeSelected will be true 'next'. Thus, we use template 41: G((bool_exp1 & bool_exp2) -> (X bool_exp3)), with bool_exp1 = PCVMode, bool_exp2 = inspiratoryPhaseEnd, and bool_exp3 = PSVModeSelected. N_DURATION is not needed for this template.

The requirement is that switching to PSV mode should occur at the end of the PCV inspiratory phase. In terms of atomic propositions, 'PCV inspiratory time ends' can be represented by 'inspiratoryPhaseEnd', and 'the switch to PSV' can be represented by 'PSVModeSelected'. To capture 'at the end of', we enforce that whenever the inspiratory phase ends, the system immediately switches to PSV in the next step. Therefore, the property is: Globally, when inspiratoryPhaseEnd occurs, in the next state PSVModeSelected must occur. This uses template 11.

The requirement states that the mode switch to PSV (Pressure Support Ventilation) shall happen at the end of the inspiratory period in PCV mode. In the atomic propositions, 'PSVModeSelected' represents the switch to PSV and 'inspiratoryPhaseEnd' represents the end of the inspiratory phase (assumed for PCV if no more specific signal available). Thus, whenever the end of the inspiratory phase occurs, this should lead to a transition (in the next step) to the PSV mode being initiated. This is naturally expressed in LTL as: Globally (if inspiratoryPhaseEnd holds, then in the next time step, PSVModeSelected).

The natural language states: 'The switch to PSV shall occur at the end of a PCV inspiratory time.' 
- Observing the atomic propositions, 'PCVMode' indicates pressure-controlled ventilation mode (in use for the inspiratory phase), 'inspiratoryPhaseEnd' marks the end of inspiration, and 'PSVModeSelected' identifies the switch to PSV mode is initiated by the user.
- However, the requirement is about the actual event of switching (not initiation by the user), which is best linked to 'PSVMode' (the state denoting that the system is actually in Pressure Support Ventilation). The desired property is thus: whenever the end of inspiratory phase (in PCV mode) occurs, the switch to 'PSV' must occur. Formally, this is best captured as: whenever (in PCV mode and at end of inspiratory phase), it implies that PSVMode becomes true (i.e., the system enters PSV mode).
- The most fitting template is index 40: G ((bool_exp1 & bool_exp2) -> bool_exp3).  
- Here, bool_exp1 = 'PCVMode' (system is in PCV), bool_exp2 = 'inspiratoryPhaseEnd' (end of inspiration), bool_exp3 = 'PSVMode' (PSV mode is active). This ensures at every occurrence of PCV mode and inspiratory phase end, the system must switch to PSV mode.  
- N_DURATION is not required in this template.

The property states that whenever the end of the PCV inspiratory phase is reached (indicated by 'inspiratoryPhaseEnd') and the system is in Pressure Controlled Ventilation mode (PCVMode), then in the next step the switch to Pressure Support Ventilation mode (PSV) should be initiated (PSVModeSelected). This directly captures the requirement: 'The switch to PSV shall occur at the end of a PCV inspiratory time.' By defining the condition (PCVMode & inspiratoryPhaseEnd) and linking it to a next-state guarantee for PSVModeSelected, the LTL expresses the required causality and timing.

The natural language states that the system should switch to Pressure Support Ventilation (PSV) at the end of a Pressure Controlled Ventilation (PCV) inspiratory time. The atomic propositions provide: 'inspiratoryPhaseEnd' to denote the end of the inspiratory phase and 'PSVModeSelected' to denote the initiation of a switch to PSV mode. The requirement is best expressed as: whenever the inspiratory phase ends, the system should immediately initiate the switch to PSV mode. Therefore, the appropriate LTL template is 'G (bool_exp2 -> (X bool_exp3))' (template ID 11), where bool_exp2 is 'inspiratoryPhaseEnd' and bool_exp3 is 'PSVModeSelected'. This enforces, at every point, that if the end of inspiratory phase occurs, the switch to PSV should occur immediately in the next step. N_DURATION is not used in this template.

The requirement is that switching to PSV (Pressure Support Ventilation) should occur at the end of a PCV (Pressure Controlled Ventilation) inspiratory time. Among the atomic propositions, 'inspiratoryPhaseEnd' represents the end of inspiratory phase—commonly marking the end of PCV inspiratory time, and 'PSVModeSelected' indicates that the switch to PSV has been initiated. To capture the transition, the most appropriate LTL property is: globally, whenever 'inspiratoryPhaseEnd' occurs (i.e., end of PCV inspiration), in the next state, 'PSVModeSelected' should occur. This matches template 11: G (bool_exp2 -> (X bool_exp3)), binding bool_exp2 to 'inspiratoryPhaseEnd' and bool_exp3 to 'PSVModeSelected'. N_DURATION is not needed for this template.

The natural language specifies that the system must switch to Pressure Support Ventilation (PSV) at the end of a Pressure-Controlled Ventilation (PCV) inspiratory time. Mapping to the available atomic propositions: 'inspiratoryPhaseEnd' denotes the end of the inspiratory phase, and 'PSVModeSelected' denotes that switching to PSV mode is initiated by the user. Therefore, the LTL property should guarantee that whenever the end of inspiratory phase occurs (i.e., 'inspiratoryPhaseEnd' is true), it is immediately followed by the initiation of the switch to PSV mode (i.e., 'PSVModeSelected' becomes true in the next step). This is captured by the template 'G (inspiratoryPhaseEnd -> X PSVModeSelected)', which ensures that on every occurrence of 'inspiratoryPhaseEnd', the next state has 'PSVModeSelected' true. This directly encodes the desired requirement from the natural language input.

The requirement is that the system should switch to PSV mode, but only at the end of a PCV inspiratory time (i.e., at the end of the inspiratory phase in PCV mode). In terms of atomic propositions, 'inspiratoryPhaseEnd' signals the end of the inspiratory time, and 'PSVModeSelected' represents the initiation of the switch to PSV mode. To formalize the requirement: whenever the switch to PSV mode is initiated, it must only happen at the end of the inspiratory phase. Thus, globally, PSVModeSelected implies inspiratoryPhaseEnd at that moment. This is best matched by template 30: G (bool_exp1 -> bool_exp3), where bool_exp1 = PSVModeSelected and bool_exp3 = inspiratoryPhaseEnd. This ensures PSV selection can only occur (or is only allowed to occur) at the end of PCV inspiration, as required.
